label: Agent CLIs
intro: Terminal-native coding agents you steer from a prompt. The day-to-day driver for spec, plan, edit, test, commit.

label: AI-native IDEs
intro: Editors and IDE extensions with agent mode built in. Inline completions, chat, multi-file edits, repo awareness.

label: Autonomous agents
intro: Longer-running agents with their own runtime, VM or browser. Take a task, walk away, review the artifact.

label: Frameworks
intro: Programmatic scaffolds for spec-driven and multi-agent systems. Code-level building blocks rather than products.

label: Memory layers
intro: Storage for what an agent should remember across runs — working, episodic, semantic, procedural.

label: Conventions
intro: Repo-level files and community standards that tell every agent how to behave inside your codebase.

label: Primitives
intro: Low-level building blocks the patterns lean on. Usually already on your machine, often overlooked.

label: Consoles & workbenches
intro: Browser-based labs for prompt design, model comparison, evals and quick prototyping.
